Class BreakpointStopCondition

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.strategy.BreakpointStopCondition
All Implemented Interfaces:
IBreakpointStopCondition, StopCondition<Goal>
public class BreakpointStopCondition extends Object implements IBreakpointStopCondition
An IBreakpointStopCondition which can be used during proof.

    Description
    BreakpointStopCondition(IBreakpoint... breakpoints)
    Creates a new BreakpointStopCondition.

    Returns all available IBreakpoints.

    getGoalNotAllowedMessage(Goal goal, int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied)
    Returns the reason why the previous check via StopCondition.isGoalAllowed(ProofGoal,int,long,long,int) has stopped the apply strategy.

    Returns the maximal amount of work needed to complete the task, used to display a progress bar.

    getStopMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
    Returns a human-readable message which explains why the previous StopCondition.shouldStop(int,long,long,int,SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.

    Checks if it is allowed to apply the next rule on the selected ProofGoal chosen by the GoalChooser before it is applied.

    Removes an IBreakpoint.

  • Constructor Details

    • BreakpointStopCondition

      public BreakpointStopCondition(IBreakpoint... breakpoints)
      Creates a new BreakpointStopCondition.
      Parameters:
      breakpoints - The IBreakpoint to use.

  • Method Details

    • getMaximalWork

      public int getMaximalWork(int maxApplications, long timeout)
      Returns the maximal amount of work needed to complete the task, used to display a progress bar. Pass 0 to indicate unknown size.
      Specified by:
      getMaximalWork in interface StopCondition<Goal>
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      Returns:
      The maximal amount of work or 0 if it is unknown.

    • isGoalAllowed

      public boolean isGoalAllowed(Goal goal, int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied)
      Checks if it is allowed to apply the next rule on the selected ProofGoal chosen by the GoalChooser before it is applied. If it is not allowed the apply strategy will stop.
      Specified by:
      isGoalAllowed in interface StopCondition<Goal>
      Parameters:
      goal - The current ProofGoal on which the next rule will be applied.
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the proof search (apply strategy) has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      Returns:
      true rule application is allowed, false rule application is not allowed so stop apply strategy

    • getGoalNotAllowedMessage

      public String getGoalNotAllowedMessage(Goal goal, int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied)
      Returns the reason why the previous check via StopCondition.isGoalAllowed(ProofGoal,int,long,long,int) has stopped the apply strategy.
      Specified by:
      getGoalNotAllowedMessage in interface StopCondition<Goal>
      Parameters:
      goal - The current ProofGoal on which the next rule will be applied.
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      Returns:
      description of the reason why automatic proof search has stopped

    • shouldStop

      public boolean shouldStop(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
      Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.
      Specified by:
      shouldStop in interface StopCondition<Goal>
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      singleRuleApplicationInfo - An optional SingleRuleApplicationInfo.
      Returns:
      true stop strategy, false continue strategy and apply next rule.

    • isBreakpointHit

      protected boolean isBreakpointHit(SourceElement activeStatement, RuleApp ruleApp, Node node)
      Checks if a breakpoint is hit.
      Parameters:
      activeStatement - the activeStatement of the node
      ruleApp - the applied RuleApp
      node - the current node
      Returns:
      true at least one breakpoint is hit, false all breakpoints are not hit.

    • getStopMessage

      public @NonNull String getStopMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
      Returns a human-readable message which explains why the previous StopCondition.shouldStop(int,long,long,int,SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.
      Specified by:
      getStopMessage in interface StopCondition<Goal>
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      singleRuleApplicationInfo - An optional SingleRuleApplicationInfo.
      Returns:
      The human-readable message which explains the stop reason.

    • addBreakpoint

      public void addBreakpoint(IBreakpoint breakpoint)
      Adds a new IBreakpoint.
      Specified by:
      addBreakpoint in interface IBreakpointStopCondition
      Parameters:
      breakpoint - The IBreakpoint to add.

    • removeBreakpoint

      public void removeBreakpoint(IBreakpoint breakpoint)
      Removes an IBreakpoint.
      Specified by:
      removeBreakpoint in interface IBreakpointStopCondition
      Parameters:
      breakpoint - The IBreakpoint to remove.

    • getBreakpoints

      public Set<IBreakpoint> getBreakpoints()
      Returns all available IBreakpoints.
      Specified by:
      getBreakpoints in interface IBreakpointStopCondition
      Returns:
      The available IBreakpoints.
